subject: Project Management
title: Continuous Integration
description: A short lesson to help you apply Continuous Integration.

hook.text: In traditional projects, teams merged their code once — at the end. It was always a disaster.

buildup.text: Continuous Integration flipped this: merge code multiple times a day, every day.

discovery.text: Automated tests run on every merge, catching bugs in minutes instead of months.

twist.text: CI requires discipline — every developer must keep the build green. One broken build blocks everyone.

climax.text: Teams with CI deploy confidently. Teams without it dread integration like a root canal.

punchline.text: Merge often and it's painless. Wait and it's agony.

quiz.question: What is the core idea behind Continuous Integration?
options: Merge code frequently and test automatically with every change
Write all code in a single file
Test only at the end of the project
